api_response.name: human diseases
api_response.description: Human diseases are health conditions that may be associated with transcriptionally competent HERV-W elements.

api_response.name: TP53 rs1042522
api_response.description: TP53 rs1042522 is a single nucleotide polymorphism in the TP53 gene associated with early pregnancy loss risk.

api_response.name: LINE-1
api_response.description: LINE-1 is an active retrotransposon comprising about 17% of the human genome and linked to genomic instability in cancer.

api_response.name: L1
api_response.description: L1 is a human retrotransposon associated with genetic instability.

api_response.name: TP53 mutation
api_response.description: TP53 mutation is a genetic alteration in the TP53 gene associated with tumor evolution and immunogenic SINE stress regulation.

api_response.name: LINE-1 ribonucleoprotein particles
api_response.description: LINE-1 ribonucleoprotein particles are complexes that bind to telomeric repeat containing RNA and support telomeric integrity in alternative lengthening of telomeres.

api_response.name: SP1
api_response.description: SP1 is a gene encoding a transcription factor that binds to specific DNA sequences.

api_response.name: HCT116
api_response.description: HCT116 is a gene associated with a human colorectal carcinoma cell line used in the study.

api_response.name: LTR7B
api_response.description: LTR7B is a transposable element integrated into the SFTPB gene.

api_response.name: AluSx
api_response.description: AluSx is a transposable element integrated into the SFTPB gene.

api_response.name: ERGIC
api_response.description: ERGIC is the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment involved in protein sorting and transport.

api_response.name: Golgi
api_response.description: Golgi is an organelle involved in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins for secretion.

api_response.name: mutation
api_response.description: Mutation is a change in the DNA sequence that can be caused by LINE-1 transposition.

api_response.name: rearrangement
api_response.description: Rearrangement is a structural alteration of genomic DNA that can result from LINE-1 transposition.

api_response.name: DLK1
api_response.description: DLK1 is a gene in the imprinted Dlk1-Dio3 cluster studied across mammalian evolution.

api_response.name: DIO3
api_response.description: DIO3 is a gene in the imprinted Dlk1-Dio3 cluster studied across mammalian evolution.

api_response.name: HDAC inhibitors
api_response.description: HDAC inhibitors are compounds that inhibit histone deacetylases and modulate gene expression.

api_response.name: ADAR inhibitors
api_response.description: ADAR inhibitors are compounds that inhibit adenosine deaminases acting on RNA to affect RNA editing.

api_response.name: D125N
api_response.description: D125N is a somatic, damaging single nucleotide variant in the PTPRQ protein.

api_response.name: PTPRQ
api_response.description: PTPRQ is a protein containing the D125N mutation identified in a well-differentiated liposarcoma.

api_response.name: tdTomato
api_response.description: tdTomato is a fluorescent protein used as a reporter in genetic studies.

api_response.name: enhanced green fluorescent protein
api_response.description: Enhanced green fluorescent protein is a fluorescent protein used as a reporter in genetic studies.

api_response.name: Condensin II
api_response.description: Condensin II is a protein complex that restricts LINE-1 retrotransposition by inhibiting transcription and translation.

api_response.name: GAIT complexes
api_response.description: GAIT complexes are protein assemblies that inhibit translation of mRNAs, including those of LINE-1 retrotransposons, in response to interferon-gamma.

api_response.name: ChAHP
api_response.description: ChAHP is a protein complex composed of ADNP, CHD4, and HP1 proteins that represses retrotransposons.

api_response.name: ChAHP2
api_response.description: ChAHP2 is a protein complex homologous to ChAHP, composed of ADNP2, CHD4, and HP1 proteins, that represses retrotransposons.

api_response.name: AGO2
api_response.description: AGO2 is a gene encoding Argonaute 2, involved in RNA interference and gene repression.

api_response.name: EIF2C2
api_response.description: EIF2C2 is a gene encoding Argonaute 2, also known as AGO2, involved in RNA-mediated gene silencing.

api_response.name: MER20
api_response.description: MER20 is a transposable element that contributes to the enhancer/promoter for decidual prolactin in humans.

api_response.name: MER39
api_response.description: MER39 is a transposable element that contributes to the enhancer/promoter for decidual prolactin in humans.

api_response.name: SVA insertion in MSH6
api_response.description: SVA insertion in MSH6 is a retrotransposon insertion in exon 5 of the MSH6 gene that causes aberrant RNA splicing and Lynch syndrome.

api_response.name: SVA insertion in MSH2
api_response.description: SVA insertion in MSH2 is a retrotransposon insertion in exon 3 of the MSH2 gene that causes aberrant RNA splicing and Lynch syndrome.

api_response.name: PRNP
api_response.description: PRNP is a gene encoding the prion protein involved in repeat expansions.

api_response.name: USH1C
api_response.description: USH1C is a gene associated with mitochondrial-nuclear sequence transfer.

api_response.name: ERCC6
api_response.description: ERCC6 is a gene where a 26-bp insertion suggests DNA oligonucleotide capture.

missing_names: Gross deletion in IGHM, BTK, Artemis

api_response.name: Gross deletion in IGHM
api_response.description: Gross deletion in IGHM is a genetic mutation involving large-scale deletion in the IGHM gene.

api_response.name: Gross deletion in BTK
api_response.description: Gross deletion in BTK is a genetic mutation involving large-scale deletion in the BTK gene.

api_response.name: Gross deletion in Artemis
api_response.description: Gross deletion in Artemis is a genetic mutation involving large-scale deletion in the Artemis gene.

api_response.name: L1(Ta)
api_response.description: L1(Ta) is a retrotransposon in the human genome that contributes to genetic diversity.

api_response.name: LINE-1
api_response.description: LINE-1 is a type of retrotransposon involved in structural genomic variation.

api_response.name: Alu SINEs
api_response.description: Alu SINEs are short interspersed nuclear elements that are sources of genomic diversity.

api_response.name: MER44
api_response.description: MER44 is a family of transposable elements enriched in differentially accessible chromatin in stimulated human macrophages.

api_response.name: THE1
api_response.description: THE1 is a family of transposable elements that harbor binding motifs for transcription factors like NF-κB in immune response contexts.

api_response.name: Tigger3
api_response.description: Tigger3 is a family of transposable elements contributing to regulatory regions near differentially expressed genes in immune activation.

api_response.name: MLT1
api_response.description: MLT1 is a family of transposable elements involved in accessible chromatin regions associated with gene expression regulation during immune responses.

missing_names: defect in ATM, MRE11, BLM, LIG4, WRN, Ku70

api_response.name: defect in ATM
api_response.description: Defect in ATM is a mutation in the ATM gene associated with impaired fertility and chromothripsis.

api_response.name: defect in MRE11
api_response.description: Defect in MRE11 is a mutation in the MRE11 gene linked to double-strand break response impairment and chromothripsis.

api_response.name: defect in BLM
api_response.description: Defect in BLM is a mutation in the BLM gene that contributes to chromosomal instability and chromothripsis.

api_response.name: defect in LIG4
api_response.description: Defect in LIG4 is a mutation in the LIG4 gene involved in DNA repair and chromothripsis susceptibility.

api_response.name: defect in WRN
api_response.description: Defect in WRN is a mutation in the WRN gene associated with premature aging and chromothripsis.

api_response.name: defect in Ku70
api_response.description: Defect in Ku70 is a mutation in the Ku70 gene that affects double-strand break response and chromothripsis.
